description: Materials (files, consent, etc.) relating to individual(s)
One or more characters used to identify, name, or characterize the nature, properties, or contents of a thing
An event (an experiment, an observation, etc.) which produces a material
A reference to one or more individuals associated to the material
Additional identifiers that identify a material
One or more associations between the record and an organisation.

Consent given by a patient to a surgical or medical procedure or participation in a study, examination or analysis after achieving an understanding of the relevant medical facts and the risks involved.

Reference to the informed consent form that was signed. Points to a particular instance of leaflet and consent form that usually exists as a record (i.e. a row) within the same database as this individual consent.
Indicates the institute who performed the collection act.
A date specification that designates when this individual consent form was signed.
Starting date of the validity of this individual consent.
End date of the validity of this individual consent.
An individual who is authorized under applicable State or local law to consent on behalf of a child or incapable person to general medical care including participation in clinical research.
A data item that is used to indicate consent permissions for datasets and/or materials, and relates to the purposes for which datasets and/or material might be used.
Data use modifiers indicate additional conditions for use. For instance, a dataset is restricted to investigations into specific diseases or performed at specific geographical locations.

Further specification of applied data use permissions and modifiers. For example, a list of countries in case of geographic restrictions or a list of diseases when restricted to disease-specific research.
The procedure of recontacting the patient for specified reasons. This means the patient agrees to be re-identifiable under those circumstances.
Information on all samples and extensions
Extension for OGM samples
Extension for RNA samples
Extension for lrGS samples
An identifier associated with a particular source, used by the data provider or owner
The type of sample as characterized by its biological source material collected for testing, diagnostic, propagation, treatment or research purposes
If the sample type is other, provide a description of the sample.
The tissue type of origin (e.g., lung or heart) for the sample
The number of aliquots (= pertaining to a portion of the whole; any one of two or more samples of something, of the same volume or weight)
The number of aliquots (= pertaining to a portion of the whole; any one of two or more samples of something, of the same volume or weight)
The number of aliquots (= pertaining to a portion of the whole; any one of two or more samples of something, of the same volume or weight)
A unique identifier assigned to an aliquot containing a portion of a biospecimen
An indication if the sample is available (i.e., retained, available for use, etc.)

An examination that is performed to determine the extent of the spread of cancer based on histopathologic examination of one or more pathology specimens. Pathologic staging uses evidence acquired during the process of clinical staging, supplemented by histopathologic examination of surgical specimens.
The determination of the volume of a sample.
An indication of the type of unit of measure being used to express a volume.

The quantity of a substance per unit volume or weight; a measure of the amount of substance present in a unit amount of mixture, particularly, the amount of solute dissolved in a solvent. The amounts can be expressed as moles, masses, volumes, or parts.
An indication of the type of unit of measure being used to express a concentration.

validation: if(parseFloat(rINQCValue) < 1 || parseFloat(rINQCValue) > 10) "Only values between 1 and 10 are allowed."
description: A data field which describes quality control information
Was a Nonsense-Mediated Decay (NMC) inhibitor added?
The percentage of tumor cells compared to total cells present in this sample.

columnName: age group of individual at collection
description: A categorical value that groups this individual by age at the time of collection (e.g., "Adult", "18-24 years", etc.)

columnName: age of individual at collection in years
description: The age of the individual (in years) at the time of collection
The specific anatomical or biological region associated with a tissue or organ specimen.
Additional description on the anatomical or biological region associated with a tissue or organ specimen
An indication confirming that quality control processes were carried out and that the sequence did not meet the assessment criteria.
An explanation why quality control failed
Date and time at which this sample was listed or recorded officially, i.e. officially qualified or enrolled.
Amount of input material in nanogram (ng).
The geographic location where sample collection took place
The person or persons responsible for sample collection
Date on which the sample was collected
The number of cells (million) in a sample, sample volume, field, or other delimiter.
A scalar measurement datum that is the result of measurement of length of a genomic fragment
An assessment of nucleic acid purity that is measured by determining the ratio of light absorbance of the nucleic acid sample at 260nm and 280nm respectively.
An assessment of nucleic acid purity that is measured by determining the ratio of light absorbance of the nucleic acid sample at 260nm and 230nm respectively.
An ionic compound that resists changes in its pH. The buffer containing the DNA/RNA.
An ionic compound that resists changes in its pH. The buffer containing the DNA/RNA.
The conditions under which this sample was stored.

A place on the Earth where this sample is located, by its name or by its geographical location. This definition is intentionally vague to allow reuse locally (e.g. which freezer), for contacting (e.g. which institute), broadly for logistical or legal reasons (e.g. city, country or continent).
The date this sample is no longer regarded as fit for use.

The quantity of a substance per unit volume or weight; a measure of the amount of substance present in a unit amount of mixture, particularly, the amount of solute dissolved in a solvent. The amounts can be expressed as moles, masses, volumes, or parts.
An indication of the type of unit of measure being used to express a concentration.
An concentration measurement is the determination of the concentration of a compound in solution. [Allotrope]

validation: if(parseFloat(dINQCValue) < 1 || parseFloat(dINQCValue) > 10) "Only values between 1 and 10 are allowed."
description: A data field which describes quality control information
Sample batch lot identifier
An electronic file is an information content entity which conforms to a specification or format and which is meant to hold data and information in digital form, accessible to software agents.

The determination of complete (typically nucleotide) sequences, including those of genomes (full genome sequencing, de novo sequencing and resequencing), amplicons and transcriptomes. In FAIR Genomes: Sequencing. Ideally: SequencingRuns.
A fixed-size datum calculated (by using a hash function) for a file
The type of checksum (e.g., MD5, SHA256)
A digital resource format (e.g. BAM, FastQ, gVCF, phenopacket, BED)
A reference to one or more resources that organises materials for in a meaningful way (e.g., data releases, subpopulations, cohorts, etc.)
The source or origin of the record. For example, an organisation, database, system, etc.
The date the record was created at the source
The person who created the record at the source
The date the record was updated at the source
The person who updated the record at the source
A written explanation, observation or criticism added to textual material
File locations refers to the specific places or paths where files are stored on a computer or within a file system
The literal identifier for an electronic file
The location a file is stored (e.g., server, building, filing cabinet, etc.)
A file path is an identifier for a file in a file system or network (e.g., a scp compatible <host>:<path>)
A reference to one or more resources that organises records for in a meaningful way (e.g., data releases, subpopulations, cohorts, etc.)
The date the record was created at the storage location
The person who created the record at the storage location
A written explanation, observation or criticism added to textual material
The storage location where a file is stored (e.g., server, building, filing cabinet, etc.)
The name that identifies the file storage location
The classification of a file storage location based on its physical or digital nature
The person responsible of or primary contact for the file storage location
The organisation where the the file storage is located
The protocol used for accessing and retrieving files from the storage location
